children.name: Varanus
children.description: Large to enormous predatory lizards with deeply forked tongues, powerful claws, and muscular bodies. Monitors actively hunt prey rather than ambushing it, using their long forked tongue to track scent trails. The Komodo Dragon is the largest lizard on Earth, capable of killing water buffalo.

children.name: Varanus komodoensis
children.description: World's largest living lizard at up to 3 m and 70 kg; venomous — venom glands disrupt blood clotting; capable of parthenogenesis; restricted to a handful of Indonesian islands; IUCN Vulnerable.

children.name: Varanus niloticus
children.description: Africa's longest lizard; powerful swimmer; uses tools to raid crocodile nests; invasive in Florida.

children.name: Varanus salvator
children.description: Second-largest lizard; strong swimmer; found near water across SE Asia; eaten and traded widely.

children.name: Varanus varius
children.description: Arboreal predator of Australian eucalypt forests; raids bird nests; climbs telegraph poles.

children.name: Varanus griseus
children.description: Wide desert range from Sahara to Central Asia; tolerates extreme heat.

children.name: Varanus exanthematicus
children.description: Stocky; popular in captivity; some populations specialise on ants and termites.

children.name: Varanus acanthurus
children.description: Small spiny-tailed species of rocky Australian outback.
